description: Progressive delivery workflow for Claude OS work. Inspired by artifact-first MVP workflows: define intent, requirements, technical plan, agent context, execute, verify, and close.

phases.title: Intent Capture
phases.purpose: Clarify what is being changed and why before reading or editing broadly.
phases.gate: User intent and constraints are explicit enough to choose a safe route.

phases.title: Requirements & Acceptance
phases.purpose: Convert intent into observable acceptance criteria and exclusions.
phases.gate: The work has a bounded outcome and known non-goals.

phases.title: Technical Plan
phases.purpose: Choose the smallest safe implementation path, validation plan, and rollback posture.
phases.gate: A reviewer can tell which files may change and how correctness will be proven.

phases.title: Agent Context Setup
phases.purpose: Load only the docs, skills, capabilities, and local context required for the task.
phases.gate: The agent has enough context to act without brute-reading the repo.

phases.title: Incremental Execution
phases.purpose: Apply one bounded change at a time while preserving compatibility.
phases.gate: Each change is reviewable and tied to an acceptance criterion.

phases.title: Verification
phases.purpose: Run the cheapest sufficient checks first, then escalate for high-risk surfaces.
phases.gate: Validation evidence exists or the gap is explicitly stated.

phases.title: Closure & Handoff
phases.purpose: Record final state, decisions, rollback notes, and next steps.
phases.gate: The next agent or human can continue without relying on chat memory.

globalRules: Use route-capability before selecting commands for non-trivial work.
Use query-docs-index before reading full documentation files.
Ask for human approval before critical or high-risk mutation.
Run verify-os-health after changes to manifests, bootstrap, skills, validators, or docs-index.
Do not expose secrets, PII, stack traces, or raw generated reports in user-facing output.
